*������������������l"""
Generic docs help button and pulse par system

DocsHelper = op.TDResources.mod.DocsHelper.DocsHelper

2
*������������������/def onPulse(par):
	parent.DocsHelper.OpenDocs()



2
*����������������� ># Text Component callbacks

def onValueChange(comp, value, prevValue):
	"""
	Called whenever the text component's value parameter changes for any reason including typing
	in the text window or changing the parameter directly. The callback is equivalent to the
	onValueChange in the parameter exectute DAT.
	
	Args:
		comp: the text component
		value: the new value
		prevValue: the previous value
	"""
	#debug('\nonValueChange comp:', comp.path, '- new value: ', value, ', prev value: ', prevValue )
	return
	
def onFocus(comp):
	"""
	Called when one of the text components viewers gets keyboard focus. 
	
	Args:
		comp: the text component
	"""
	#debug('\nonFocus comp:', comp.path)
	return

def onFocusEnd(comp, info):
	"""
	Called when one of the text component's viewers loses keyboard focus. In regular editing mode
	this is right after the contents are saved back to the value parameter. This function is called
	everytime the viewer loses focus, even if the text does not change.
	
	Args:
		comp: the text component
		info: a dictionary containing information about the event including:
			reason: a string indicating what caused the viewer to lose focus e.g. 'enter', 'escape', 'tab', 'unknown'
	
	"""
	#debug('\nonFocusEnd comp:', comp.path, '- info:\n', info)
	return
	
def onTextEdit(comp):
	"""
	Called each time the contents of the text component viewer change i.e. when typing a character,
	deleting text, cutting or pasting. 
	
	Args:
		comp: the text component
	"""
	#debug('\nonTextEdit comp:', comp.path, '- text: ', comp.editText)
	return
	
def onTextEditEnd(comp, value, prevValue):
	"""
	Called when the user finishes editing and the value has been changed. Not called in 'Continuous Edit' mode. Use
	onTextEdit and onFocusEnd when using continuous editing.

    Note: Any Python undo blocks created by this callback will be included in the undo step for the change to the text field.
	
	Args:
		comp: the text component
		value: the new value
		prevValue: the previous value
	"""
	#debug('\nonTextEditEnd comp:', comp.path, '- new value: ', value, ', prev value: ', prevValue )
	return



2
*�����������������+# me - this DAT
# logger - the logger COMP to which this callback DAT is attached

def onMessageLogged(info):
	"""
	onMessageLogged() is called once everytime a message is logged.

	info - A dict with the following keys
		logItemDict - the referenced COMP of the parameter Comptoexport

	"""
	return

def onPulse(par):

Generic docs help button and pulse par system

2
*������������������/def onPulse(par):
	parent.DocsHelper.OpenDocs()



2
*������������������# me - this DAT.
# 
# dat - the DAT that received the error
# rowIndex - the row number the error was placed into
# message - the error message
# absFrame - the absolute frame at which the error occured
# frame - the local frame at which the error occured
# severity - describes the level of error (0=info, 1=warning, 2=error, 3=fatal)
# type - describes the family of error
# source - the operator that generated the error

def onError(dat, rowIndex, message, absFrame, frame, severity, type, source):
	return

2
*�����������������+# me - this DAT
# logger - the logger COMP to which this callback DAT is attached

def onMessageLogged(info):
	"""
	onMessageLogged() is called once everytime a message is logged.

	info - A dict with the following keys
		logItemDict - the referenced COMP of the parameter Comptoexport

	"""
	return



2
*����������������� # me - this DAT.
# webrtcDAT - the conext WebRTCExt DAT
# connectionId - uuid of the connection associated with the callback

def onOffer(webrtcDAT, connectionId, localSdp):
	webrtcDAT.setLocalDescription(connectionId, 'offer', localSdp, stereo=True)
	ext.WebRTCPanelExt.onMessageSendingOffer(
		connectionId,
		localSdp
	)
	return

def onAnswer(webrtcDAT, connectionId, localSdp):
	webrtcDAT.setLocalDescription(connectionId, 'answer', localSdp, stereo=True)
	ext.WebRTCPanelExt.onMessageSendingAnswer(
		connectionId,
		localSdp
	)
	return

def onNegotiationNeeded(webrtcDAT, connectionId):
	"""
		From MDN: Once the caller has created its
		RTCPeerConnection, created a media stream, 
		and added its tracks to the connection as shown in Starting a call,
		the browser will deliver a negotiationneeded event to the RTCPeerConnection to indicate 
		that it's ready to begin negotiation with the other peer.
	"""
	ext.WebRTCPanelExt.onNegotiationNeeded(connectionId)
	return

def onIceCandidate(webrtcDAT, connectionId, candidate, lineIndex, sdpMid):
	ext.WebRTCPanelExt.onMessageSendingIce(
		connectionId,
		candidate,
		lineIndex, 
		sdpMid
	)
	return

def onIceCandidateError(webrtcDAT, connectionId, errorText):
	ext.WebRTCPanelExt.Logger.Error(f'ICE Candidate Error {connectionId, errorText}')
	return

def onTrack(webrtcDAT, connectionId, trackId, type):
	'''
	Adding a track to a connection triggers renegotiation by firing a negotiationneeded
	'''
	ext.WebRTCPanelExt.Logger.Info(f'onTrack was called with args {connectionId} {trackId} {type}')
	ext.WebRTCPanelExt.onTrack(connectionId, trackId, type)
	return

def onRemoveTrack(webrtcDAT, connectionId, trackId, type):
	ext.WebRTCPanelExt.onRemoveTrack(connectionId, trackId, type)
	return

def onDataChannel(webrtcDAT, connectionId, channelName):
	ext.WebRTCPanelExt.onDataChannel(connectionId, channelName)
	return

def onDataChannelOpen(webrtcDAT, connectionId, channelName):
	ext.WebRTCPanelExt.onDataChannelOpen(connectionId, channelName)
	return

def onDataChannelClose(webrtcDAT, connectionId, channelName):
	ext.WebRTCPanelExt.onDataChannelClose(connectionId, channelName)
	return

def onData(webrtcDAT, connectionId, channelName, data):
	ext.WebRTCPanelExt.onDataReceived(connectionId, channelName, data)
	return
	
def onConnectionStateChange(webrtcDAT, connectionId, newState):
	ext.WebRTCPanelExt.Logger.Info(f'{connectionId} Connection state changed to {newState}')
	if newState == 'closed':
		ext.WebRTCPanelExt.onConnectionStateChangeClosed(connectionId)

	return

def onSignalingStateChange(webrtcDAT, connectionId, newState):
	ext.WebRTCPanelExt.Logger.Info(f'{connectionId} Signaling state changed to {newState}')
	return

def onIceConnectionStateChange(webrtcDAT, connectionId, newState):
	ext.WebRTCPanelExt.Logger.Info(f'{connectionId} ICE connection state changed to {newState}')
	if newState == 'failed':
		webrtcDAT.restartIce(connectionId)

	return

def onIceGatheringStateChange(webrtcDAT, connectionId, newState):
	ext.WebRTCPanelExt.Logger.Info(f'{connectionId} Gathering state changed to {newState}')
	return
